title: Inter-process Communication and Mutual Exclusion
summary: Covers IPC via shared memory, the mutual exclusion problem, semaphores, monitors, and thread communication risks.
content_points: Shared memory IPC: same segment in multiple descriptor tables or same page frame in multiple page tables.
OS controls shared zones; processes are aware of shared nature.
Mutual exclusion: at most one process accesses a shared resource at any time.
Semaphore: simplest synchronisation primitive; managed by the OS via system calls.
Interference risk in threads: global variables are automatically shared → race conditions.
Programmer's responsibility: use semaphores correctly at low level.
learning_objectives: Describe IPC via shared memory (segmentation and pagination mechanisms)
Explain mutual exclusion and the semaphore concept
Identify race conditions in threaded programs
